reforms.description: The Polish government has initiated a re-examination of social policy priorities to better target support for vulnerable groups and recognized the need for integrated rural development policies to address challenges faced by rural areas, including the promotion of high-value added and high-technology companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on high-value and high-technology companies is expected to enhance economic resilience and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms represent significant but narrower changes in policy focus.
reforms.source_quote: Snowballing budget costs of transfers and their inefficiency in supporting the most needy have led to a re-examination of social policy priorities at the end of the 1990s.

reforms.description: The authorities launched a Coal Sector Reform Programme aimed at orderly mine closures, employment downsizing, and the re-launch of profitable enterprises, supported by a $1 billion loan from the World Bank.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to transition the coal sector towards profitability and sustainability, which can enhance economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into action and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the coal sector with significant implications for the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Coal Sector Reform Programme 1998-2002 was introduced for orderly mine closures, employment downsizing and the re-launch of profitable enterprises.

reforms.description: The government is pursuing reforms to facilitate more financing for start-ups and SMEs, addressing their financial disadvantages and aiming to improve access to capital for innovative firms, alongside the Government Policy Guidelines for SMEs until 2002 which aims to improve capital formation, domestic competitiveness, and export performance through technology transfers and vocational training for entrepreneurs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to capital and support for SMEs is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been executed and are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms are significant in supporting the startup ecosystem but do not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The aim is to improve their capital formation, domestic competitiveness and export performance via technology transfers by the Technology Agency.

reforms.description: The Polish government approved a programme in July 2000 aimed at increasing the innovativeness of the economy until 2006, which seeks to reformulate ongoing and new innovation-related policies in an integrated framework, alongside the new Act on Telecommunications effective from January 2001, which abolishes the exclusive rights of the public telecommunications operator and introduces competitive licensing to enhance competition and innovation in the telecommunications sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The integrated framework and competitive licensing are expected to foster innovation and improve the overall economic environment.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of innovation policy in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: The new Act on Telecommunications, which came into force in January 2001, abolishes the exclusive rights enjoyed by the public telecommunications operator TPSA.
